title: Computational Thinking
text:
Welcome to the course website for Computational Thinking taught at FHV – Vorarlberg University of Applied Sciences.
This site is your central entry point for course information, learning materials, and updates throughout the semester.

title: Computational Thinking
text:
Welcome to the course website for Computational Thinking taught at FHV – Vorarlberg University of Applied Sciences.
This site is your central entry point for course information, learning materials, and updates throughout the semester.

title: Computational Thinking
text:
Who this course is for
This course is designed for undergraduate students in:

Informatics / Computer Science
Information Systems

No advanced background is required beyond basic analytical thinking and motivation to solve real-world problems in a structured way.

title: Computational Thinking
text:
What you will learn
In this course, you will learn how to:

break complex problems into manageable parts (decomposition)
identify patterns and reusable solution strategies
model systems using suitable abstractions
design and evaluate algorithmic solutions
communicate technical reasoning clearly

title: Computational Thinking
text:
How to use this site
Use the navigation to access:

the syllabus and course organization
lecture-related materials
assignment and exercise information
important announcements

title: Computational Thinking
text:
Course focus
The course connects computational methods with practical applications from software engineering and information systems, helping you build a strong foundation for later technical courses and projects.
